title: fragaria vesca subvar. crenata
intro: Fragaria vesca subvar. crenata, also known as the woodland strawberry or serrated-leaf strawberry, is a subvariety of the wild strawberry plant native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and protect against infections. The health benefits of Fragaria vesca subvar. crenata include its ability to support immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and promote overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant can be consumed as a tea, added to salads, or made into a jam or syrup, and its leaves and flowers can also be used in infusions and tinctures.
